summary: I do not see any remaining consumer-side findings in this round. The `clickable/button` scorer now gives `r9-aria-role` controls a decisive button bias, which closes the residual `clickable/link` regression from round 5, and the regression test was tightened to assert the exact expected pattern instead of only asserting `not pill`. Within the consumer layer, that resolves the rounds 1-5 classification issues you asked about. The only meaningful residual risk I can still see is the already-deferred producer-side role flattening (`r9-aria-role` collapsing button/link/tab), not a blocker for this
strengths: `services/canvas-engine/src/pattern-library/patterns/clickable-button.ts` now explicitly routes `r9-aria-role` controls toward `clickable/button`, which fixes the exact misclassification path from the
